title: quercus cerris
intro: Quercus cerris, also known as the Turkish oak or Hungarian oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Central Europe. The bark and leaves of the Quercus cerris plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus cerris include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Quercus cerris is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with certain medications.
